user_query:
I'm an international scholarship recipient studying at the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland, and I need to prepare formal meeting notes and action items for a scholarship renewal planning meeting.

Please use the inputs in input/ to create three deliverables in the output/ directory:
1) output/guidance_extracted.json
2) output/action_items.csv
3) output/meeting_notes.md

Data sources and required use:
- Parse input/scholarship_guidance.html to extract:
  a) Important dates (Application Opens, Submission Deadline, Decision Notification)
  b) Required documents (name, due_date, category, and the description text)
- Read input/participants.csv to populate the Attendees list and to assign owners for tasks.
- Read input/agenda.md and include its bullet points as the Agenda Summary.
- Inspect input/notes_template.yaml to enforce the exact required sections in meeting_notes.md and to enforce the exact column order for action_items.csv.
- Read input/meeting_info.yaml for the Meeting Title, Date, and Location.

Deliverable details:
A) output/guidance_extracted.json
- JSON with two top-level keys:
  {
    "important_dates": {
      "application_opens": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "submission_deadline": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "decision_notification": "YYYY-MM-DD"
    },
    "required_documents": [
      {"name": "...", "due_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "category": "student|mentor|administrative", "description": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
- Values must be extracted from input/scholarship_guidance.html only.

B) output/action_items.csv
- Columns and order must match action_item_columns in input/notes_template.yaml exactly: task, owner, due_date, source, category.
- Create one row per required document extracted from input/scholarship_guidance.html with:
  - task = "Prepare: <document name>"
  - owner determined by category using participants.csv:
    • category "student" → first participant whose role contains "Student"
    • category "mentor" → first participant whose role contains "Mentor"
    • category "administrative" → first participant whose role contains "Administrator"
    If no match for a category, default owner is the first participant whose role contains "Student".
  - due_date = the document's due_date from the HTML
  - source = "scholarship_guidance"
  - category = the document's category
- Add one additional planning action item based on the agenda: task = "Schedule check-in meeting", owner = the participant whose role contains "Mentor", due_date = (Submission Deadline − 7 days), source = "agenda", category = "planning".

C) output/meeting_notes.md
- Must include the sections exactly as listed in required_sections in input/notes_template.yaml, in any reasonable order, but every section must be present and labeled with the same section names:
  - Meeting Title
  - Date
  - Location
  - Attendees
  - Agenda Summary
  - Scholarship Requirements Summary
  - Decisions
  - Action Items
- Populate metadata fields from input/meeting_info.yaml.
- Attendees: list names from input/participants.csv in the CSV file order.
- Agenda Summary: include the bullet points from input/agenda.md.
- Scholarship Requirements Summary: list each required document with its due date (from guidance_extracted.json) in a concise line-per-item format.
- Decisions: include a line committing to submit all materials before the Submission Deadline and specify the check-in meeting date computed as (Submission Deadline − 7 days).
- Action Items: summarize the tasks from output/action_items.csv in readable bullet points that reflect the same task titles, owners, and due dates.

General constraints:
- Do not invent attendees or documents; derive all content from the provided inputs.
- Use input/notes_template.yaml to validate both the required note sections and the CSV column order.
- Write all three files to the specified output/ paths.
- Use only the provided local files; no Internet access is required. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
